evals.prompt: Use modern-angular-architect. Analyze this Angular repo and plan how to add an orders feature with its own route, page, API service, and local models. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: A repo-specific architecture snapshot plus a concrete file-by-file plan for a routed feature, including route entrypoint, page/shell, data-access, models, implementation sequence, and explicit risks or assumptions.

evals.prompt: I want to add a reusable status badge to the invoices area of this Angular app. Use modern-angular-architect and tell me exactly where the files should go and why. Do not change any files.
evals.expected_output: A plan that keeps the component inside the invoices feature unless the repo proves broader reuse, with concrete paths, responsibilities, and rationale.

evals.prompt: Use modern-angular-architect. This repo mixes older NgModules and newer standalone code. Plan how to add a notifications feature without forcing a migration, and explain what to modify.
evals.expected_output: A mixed-architecture-aware plan that follows the nearest existing convention, avoids broad rewrites, and clearly marks new versus modified files.

evals.prompt: Analyze this nearly empty Angular repo and plan a modern feature structure for adding authentication with route protection. Use modern-angular-architect in plan mode only.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield-friendly modern Angular plan using feature-oriented structure, clear core/shared boundaries only where justified, and explicit note of assumptions.
